title: Xsa vr lora ttt
author: Himanshukkk
quantization: int8 per-row quantization with float16 scales

data.description: Cross-Sliding Attention with local sliding window and cross-summary attention streams combined via learned scalar gate
Each layer mixes value tensor from previous layer via learned scalar gate for soft dense connectivity
Relative distance bias allowing evaluation at 3x training context length without fine-tuning
Embedding factorization into vocab×64 and 64×dim matrices with tied input/output projections
Self-distillation starting at 70% training budget using model snapshot as teacher with softened KL loss

parameters.objective: minimize bpb on held-out calibration set

data.method: int8 per-row quantization with float16 scales
data.scope: all except norm, scale, gate, bias parameters

novel_contributions: Cross-Sliding Attention (XSA) combining local sliding window attention with cross-summary attention via learned gating
Value Residual connections mixing value tensors from previous layers for soft dense connectivity
Use of ALiBi positional encoding enabling 3x longer evaluation context without fine-tuning
Factorized tied embeddings reducing embedding parameters and redirecting savings to deeper layers
Self-distillation training starting at 70% of training budget to smooth predictions without external teacher
LoRA-based test-time training (TTT) on Q and V projections with low-rank adapters for efficient adaptation
N-gram bigram mixer at evaluation to handle exact repetitions and proper nouns with zero artifact cost
Temperature calibration optimized for bits-per-byte metric rather than perplexity
INT8 per-row quantization with selective float16 parameters and zlib compression to fit under 16MB
